documents.text: PostgreSQL 13 to 15 upgrade via pg_upgrade is supported but requires careful planning. Breaking changes include: removal of old replication slot behavior for logical replication, stricter SCRAM-SHA-256 authentication defaults (md5 auth deprecated), adminpack extension removed from contrib, and changes to JSON path query functions. Applications using psycopg2 2.8 or older may fail SSL handshake with PG15. SQLAlchemy 1.3 is not fully compatible with PG15 — upgrade to SQLAlchemy 1.4+ or 2.0. Review all custom PL/pgSQL functions using deprecated JSON operators (-> vs ->>).

PostgreSQL configuration risks during major version upgrades: wal_level, max_connections, shared_buffers, and autovacuum settings often need retuning after upgrade. Replication lag can spike during cutover if standby nodes are not pre-synced. Extensions such as pg_stat_statements, PostGIS, and pg_cron must be upgraded separately using ALTER EXTENSION. pg_hba.conf authentication method changes can block application connections silently. Always run pg_upgrade --check before actual migration and validate backup/restore in staging.

psycopg2 version compatibility with PostgreSQL: psycopg2 2.8.x supports PG 9.5-12 only. psycopg2 2.9.x adds support for PG 13-16 and SCRAM-SHA-256 authentication. When upgrading PostgreSQL to version 15 or 16, ensure psycopg2 is at least 2.9.3. Consider migrating to psycopg3 (psycopg package) for native async support and better PG 16 compatibility. Binary package psycopg2-binary should only be used in development — production deployments should build from source.

ETL pipeline integration risks during PostgreSQL upgrades: COPY command behavior changes between major versions can affect bulk load performance. Foreign Data Wrappers (FDW) must be recompiled against new PG version. Airflow DAGs using direct psycopg2 connections need connection string updates if authentication method changes. Reporting services using ODBC/JDBC drivers need driver version validation. Consider running parallel read replicas during migration window for zero-downtime ETL.

PostgreSQL 15 to 16 upgrade notes: PG 16 changes default SCRAM-SHA-256 channel binding to tls-server-end-point. locale provider 'builtin' added as default for new databases — existing databases keep libc. pg_stat_activity now shows query_id by default. Parallel query improvements may change query plans — run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on critical queries. Extension compatibility: pgvector 0.5+ required, pg_partman 5.0+ required. ICU collation is now the default for new installations, which may affect string sorting behavior.
